concept: Brujah union organizer turned smuggler who runs Tailored Dreams as a front while maintaining his activist ideals and serving the Camarilla's watch on the Sabbat.
appearance:
Rey Gonzalez looks like a man who's spent his life on factory floors and in union halls—heavy-set with the solid build of someone who's worked with his hands, muscle layered under the comfortable weight of middle age. His Hispanic features reflect generations of family in the Valley, with dark eyes that can shift from warm and approachable to intensely focused in an instant. His short, dark hair is kept neat and professional, and his face shows the lines of someone who's spent years organizing, negotiating, and fighting for causes he believes in.

He dresses in the practical, working-class professional style of someone who knows how to move between a business meeting and a factory floor: chinos in muted earth tones, polo shirts that suggest casual authority without pretension, and when he's on-site or handling logistics, a hard hat that he wears with the confidence of someone who's earned the right to be there. His hands are strong and capable, the kind that can shake a union member's hand with genuine warmth or grip a steering wheel for hours on a long haul.

What strikes people most about Rey is how he can shift his presence—in his tailoring shop, he's the professional business owner, calm and measured. But when he's working a crowd or making a point, that Brujah intensity surfaces: his voice gains weight and resonance, his gestures become more animated, and his eyes lock onto you with the kind of focus that makes you feel like you're the only person in the room. He knows how to read a room, when to be the calm professional, and when to let that fire show—and he uses both with the skill of someone who's spent decades learning how to move people.
biography:
Rey Gonzalez spent two decades as a union organizer in the manufacturing sector, working the line at a bag and purse factory in Phoenix. He was one of the few men on the floor, and his passion for workers' rights and his natural charisma made him a natural leader. Despite his dedication, his organizing efforts repeatedly failed—the companies always found ways to break the unions, and Rey watched his colleagues lose their jobs and hope time after time. His frustration grew, but so did his network of contacts in transportation and industry.

Brenda Wise, a Brujah from Tucson who ran a similar operation, saw potential in Rey's combination of idealism and practical connections. She embraced him in his mid-forties, recognizing that his Transportation and Industry influences would be valuable assets. Rey's Embrace didn't extinguish his activist fire—if anything, it gave him new tools to fight for what he believed in, even if the battlefield had changed.

Now Rey operates Tailored Dreams, a legitimate tailoring and alterations business in Ahwatukee that serves as a front for his smuggling operation. Most of his work involves moving goods across borders to avoid tariffs and taxes—electronics, textiles, small luxury items. Occasionally, he handles Kindred-related items that need to move discreetly. The location is strategic: Ahwatukee's proximity to Guadalupe, where the Sabbat are suspected to have a stronghold, makes it perfect for surveillance.

Rey is part of a four-person coterie under the Sheriff's direct supervision, tasked with watching the area and reporting any suspicious activity. He maintains his idealistic nature, seeing his work as protecting the Camarilla and the Masquerade—the ultimate form of protecting the people. His smuggling operation funds his lifestyle and his coterie's operations, but he still finds ways to help the working class, using his influence to get people jobs or move goods that help struggling families.

Despite his age at Embrace, Rey has adapted well to unlife. His experience as an organizer translates perfectly to managing a smuggling network, and his Brujah passion makes him a fierce defender of his territory. He keeps in touch with his sire Brenda, who runs a similar operation in Tucson, and they occasionally collaborate on larger jobs that span both cities.
equipment: Professional tailoring equipment and supplies, secure storage units for smuggled goods, multiple vehicles (delivery van, personal truck), encrypted communication devices, hidden compartments in vehicles, business records (legitimate and coded), union memorabilia from his mortal days, contact lists for transportation and industry connections.

backgroundDetails.Resources: Tailored Dreams generates legitimate income, but the smuggling operation provides the real money. Rey maintains a comfortable lifestyle and can fund coterie operations when needed.
backgroundDetails.Mentor: Brenda Wise, his sire in Tucson, continues to guide him in both Kindred politics and the practical aspects of running a smuggling operation. They collaborate on larger jobs.
backgroundDetails.Status: Recognized within the Camarilla as a reliable operator. His position in the Sheriff's coterie grants him some respect, though his age at Embrace and his background keep him from higher status.
backgroundDetails.Contacts: Extensive network in transportation (truckers, shipping companies, border agents) and industry (factory managers, union members, suppliers). These contacts are essential for his smuggling operation.
backgroundDetails.Allies: Key members of his old union network who still trust him, plus a few Kindred who appreciate his practical approach to problems. His coterie members are his closest allies.
backgroundDetails.Herd: A small group of regular customers and employees at Tailored Dreams who provide easy feeding without drawing attention. Rey is careful not to overfeed from any single source.
backgroundDetails.Influence: Transportation (4) - Controls significant influence over shipping routes, trucking companies, and border logistics. Industry (4) - Strong connections in manufacturing, unions, and industrial supply chains. These influences are the foundation of his smuggling operation.
backgroundDetails.Retainers: A trusted ghoul who helps manage the day-to-day operations of Tailored Dreams and coordinates some of the smuggling logistics. The ghoul is a former union member who Rey helped when he was mortal.

merits_flaws.name: Iron Will
merits_flaws.description: Rey's decades of organizing and his unshakeable ideals make him resistant to manipulation. His willpower is legendary among those who know him.

merits_flaws.name: Influence (Transportation)
merits_flaws.description: Rey controls significant influence over transportation networks, shipping routes, and border logistics—essential for his smuggling operation.

merits_flaws.name: Influence (Industry)
merits_flaws.description: Strong connections in manufacturing, unions, and industrial supply chains. These contacts are the foundation of both his mortal organizing work and his current operations.

merits_flaws.name: Code of Honor
merits_flaws.description: Rey maintains a strict code about who he helps and how. He refuses to smuggle anything that would directly harm working-class people, and he always keeps his word to those he considers allies.

merits_flaws.name: Frenzy Prone (Brujah Weakness)
merits_flaws.description: Like all Brujah, Rey is prone to frenzy when his ideals are challenged or he is insulted. His activist nature makes him particularly susceptible to rage when he sees injustice.

status.notes: Part of a 4-person coterie under the Sheriff's supervision, watching for Sabbat activity in Guadalupe. Tailored Dreams location in Ahwatukee is strategically chosen for proximity to suspected Sabbat stronghold. Maintains regular contact with sire Brenda Wise in Tucson. His smuggling operation mostly handles goods to avoid tariffs/taxes, with occasional Kindred-related items. Despite his criminal activities, Rey maintains his idealistic activist nature and uses his influence to help working-class people when possible. His age at Embrace (mid-40s) gives him maturity and experience that younger neonates lack, but also means he's set in his ways and less adaptable than some.

specializations.Subterfuge: Cover operations and maintaining legitimate fronts

research_notes.npc_role: Smuggler and coterie member who provides transportation/logistics services to the coterie and other Kindred. His business serves as a front and a surveillance point near the suspected Sabbat stronghold in Guadalupe. Can provide information about border activity, transportation networks, and industrial connections.
story_hooks: A shipment Rey is moving contains something dangerous that he doesn't know about—perhaps a Kindred artifact or weapon that the Sabbat are tracking.
One of Rey's old union contacts discovers his Kindred nature and threatens to expose him unless he helps with a labor dispute.
The Sheriff's coterie discovers evidence of Sabbat activity in Guadalupe, and Rey's smuggling routes become critical for moving Camarilla assets into position.
Brenda Wise (Rey's sire) arrives from Tucson with information about a larger smuggling operation that spans both cities, requiring Rey's cooperation.
Rey's idealistic nature conflicts with a coterie decision that he sees as unjust, potentially causing him to break ranks or go against orders.
research_notes.coterie_notes: Rey is part of a 4-person coterie under the Sheriff's direct supervision. The coterie's primary mission is surveillance of the suspected Sabbat stronghold in Guadalupe. Rey's role combines his smuggling operation with intelligence gathering, using his transportation and industry contacts to monitor unusual activity in the area.
